context.summary: Three distance-transform-based losses were evaluated. Only MONAI's built-in HausdorffDTLoss will be registered. The other two are deferred/not-planned.

loss.name: hausdorff_dt
loss.precomputation: None — computes EDT inline in forward()
loss.action: Register in loss factory as LIBRARY loss. Zero custom code.
loss.note: Computes distance transform on BOTH prediction and ground truth inline. Uses @torch.no_grad() for DT computation; gradients flow through (P-G)^2 term. Patch safety is limited (EDT on patches has boundary artifacts) but acceptable for evaluation and as a training regularizer.

loss.name: boundary
loss.reason-not-planned: 1. Paper is from 2019 — well-established but old approach 2. Requires dataloader-level signed DT precomputation (infrastructure burden) 3. Unbounded output range requires careful alpha scheduling 4. MONAI has HausdorffDTLoss which solves the same class of problem 5. Kervadec's approach requires BatchAwareLoss protocol extension to trainer 6. Implementation effort disproportionate to marginal benefit over MONAI built-in
loss.action: Close #100 as not-planned. Update issue description for audit trail.

loss.name: gsl
loss.reason-not-planned: 1. Bounded [0,1] output is nice but not critical for current experiments 2. Same dataloader infrastructure burden as Boundary Loss 3. Requires dataset-level class weight precomputation 4. Can be revisited if HausdorffDTLoss proves insufficient (P3-low)
loss.action: Mark #101 as P3-low, not-planned. Update issue description.

context.comparison:
For the current MiniVess experiments, MONAI's HausdorffDTLoss provides: - Zero-infrastructure surface distance optimization (no dataloader changes) - Direct MONAI import (library-first rule) - Standard forward(input, target) interface (no trainer changes needed) - Proven implementation with 3+ years of community testing
If future experiments show HausdorffDTLoss is insufficient for HD95/MASD optimization, the Celaya GSL (bounded, patch-safe) would be the next step. The existing plan at boundary-loss-implementation-plan.md is preserved as a reference for that future work.

task.name: Register MONAI HausdorffDTLoss + LogHausdorffDTLoss
task.description: Add hausdorff_dt and log_hausdorff_dt to build_loss_function(). Direct import from monai.losses — ZERO custom code. Add both to _LIBRARY_LOSSES frozenset (no warning emitted). Standard MONAI interface: forward(input, target). Parameters: alpha=2.0, softmax=softmax, to_onehot_y=to_onehot_y, include_background=False.

task.name: Experiment config update
task.description: New experiment config for surface-distance ablation: - dice_ce (baseline) - hausdorff_dt (MONAI direct — new) - cbdice_cldice (current default) No precompute_targets needed (HausdorffDTLoss computes EDT inline). 3 folds × 100 epochs.

followup.issue: CRITICAL: Next branch/PR after this task. Must close #343 and verify: 1. ModelAdapter ABC does not over-wrap MONAI models 2. Any monai.networks.nets.* model wrappable in less than 30 lines 3. monai.losses.* work without adaptation (proven by hausdorff_dt here) 4. monai.transforms.* work without adaptation 5. Re-add SegResNet, SwinUNETR, AttentionUnet, UNETR as thin wrappers Create P0 issue — MONAI ecosystem compatibility is the #1 DevEx priority.

issue.title: boundary-losses: Experiment config for surface-distance ablation

close.note: MONAI HausdorffDTLoss covers this use case. Kervadec approach (2019) requires significant dataloader infrastructure. See boundary-losses.xml for full analysis.

close.label: P3-low
close.note: Bounded GSL is interesting but deferred. MONAI HausdorffDTLoss is sufficient for current experiments. See boundary-losses.xml for full analysis.
